name: Bragi
description: Bragi is the Norse god of poetry, music, and eloquence. He is the son of Odin and the giantess Gunnlöð, making him a member of the Æsir. Bragi is renowned for his wisdom, his silver tongue, and his mastery of skaldic verse. He is often depicted as an old man with a long, flowing beard, holding a harp or lyre. Bragi's primary role is as the court poet of Valhalla, where he composes verses and entertains the fallen warriors with tales of their heroic deeds. He is considered the patron god of poets and musicians, inspiring them with his divine talent. Bragi is also the husband of Iðunn, the goddess of youth and keeper of the apples of immortality. His abilities extend beyond mere composition; he is said to possess the power to imbue his words with magical properties, influencing events and shaping the destinies of mortals. Bragi's presence signifies creativity, inspiration, and the enduring power of art. Although not a warrior god, his influence is significant, as he shapes the perception and memory of the gods and heroes through his immortal verse.
subtitle: God of Poetry, Music, and Eloquence

keyMyths.title: Bragi's Origins
keyMyths.description: Bragi is born from the union of Odin and Gunnlöð. Odin seduces Gunnlöð to obtain the mead of poetry, which he then shares with the gods, leading to Bragi's birth and his inherent poetic abilities.

keyMyths.title: Bragi and Loki's Flyting
keyMyths.description: In Lokasenna, Loki engages in a verbal duel with the gods. Bragi attempts to defend the honor of the gods, but Loki mocks him for his perceived lack of courage and skill in battle.

keyMyths.title: Bragi in Valhalla
keyMyths.description: Bragi welcomes fallen warriors to Valhalla with his poetry and music. He helps them adjust to their new lives in the hall of Odin, ensuring their stories are remembered and celebrated.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A major source for Norse mythology, including information on Bragi's role and attributes.
sources.text: A major source for Norse mythology, including information on Bragi's role and attributes.

sources.author: Unknown
